title: Iran war fallout forces Singapore Airlines-backed Air India to cancel 27% of international flights
summary_full: Air India, a carrier co-owned by Tata Group and Singapore Airlines, plans to scale back over a quarter of its international flights from June as rising costs bite.
summary_public: Air India, a carrier co-owned by Tata Group and Singapore Airlines, plans to scale back over a quarter of its international flights from June as rising costs bite.

IR.label: negative
IR.evidence: Iran war fallout forces... to cancel 27% of international flights
